title: Before you publish
assignment: Review the concepts from this section. If something is unclear, go back to the lesson.
groups.0.title: The phone
groups.0.items.0.def: <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1"> in <head>. Without it a phone pretends to be a 980-pixel screen and shrinks everything — letters like ants. width=device-width tells it the truth. It does not fix the design (that is CSS) — but without it CSS never even starts. Google looks at the site through a phone.
groups.1.title: Sharing
groups.1.items.0.def: <meta property="og:title / og:description / og:image / og:url"> — how the link looks in a chat. property, not name (everyone gets it wrong once). og:image is the most important: no image — a grey strip, an image — half a screen. The image address FULL (https://), because Facebook is not on your site. All of it in the head.
groups.2.title: The whole course, at once
groups.2.items.0.def: Skeleton (DOCTYPE, lang, charset, viewport, title). Meaning (header, nav, main, article, footer — not div soup). Content by importance (one h1, then h2). Media with a voice (alt). Data in a table (th, caption). A form that works for everyone (label with for↔id, a real button). One rule under all of it: say what a thing IS, not how it looks. The most important work in HTML is invisible — it is for the three who do not look at the screen: the blind person, Google, and you six months from now.
